properties.webBody:
This Minor Flood Warning is being issued for the Fifteen Mile Creek at Greta South.

Moderate rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Monday has caused significant river level rises.

Minor flooding is occurring along the Fifteen Mile Creek at Greta South.

Fifteen Mile Creek: 

Minor flooding is occurring at Greta South.

The Fifteen Mile Creek at Greta South peaked at 2.74 m around 02:00 am Thursday, below the minor flood level. The creek is currently at 3.14 m and falling, with minor flooding. The Fifteen Mile Creek at Greta South is likely to remain above the minor flood level (2.80 m) Friday morning.

Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do: 
  • Be prepared to act if your situation changes.
  • Stay informed by listening to emergency broadcasters and monitoring warnings.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Floodwater is toxic - never play or swim in floodwater.
Impacts in your area: 
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams.
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater.
  • Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 03/07/2026 11:00 am or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226

properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for the kiewa River at Bandiana.

NEAR MODERATE FLOODING OCCURRING AT BANDIANA

MINOR FLOODING OCCURRING AT MONGANS BRIDGE AND KIEWA

Moderate to heavy rainfall has been recorded across the Kiewa River catchment over the past few days, resulting in river level rises along the Kiewa River. Minor flooding is occurring at Mongans Bridge and Kiewa, and near moderate flooding is occurring at Bandiana.

Kiewa River downstream of Mongans Bridge:

Near moderate flooding is occurring at Bandiana, and minor flooding is occurring at Kiewa.

The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is currently at 3.41 m and steady, with minor flooding. The Kiewa River Channel at Kiewa is likely to remain above the minor flood level (3.30 m) for the remainder of Friday and into Saturday.

The Kiewa River at Bandiana is currently at 3.03 m and steady, slightly below the moderate flood level. The Kiewa River at Bandiana may reach around the moderate flood level (3.10 m) Friday morning.

Stay safe - avoid the flooded area. Be aware of potential hazards caused by flooding and never enter floodwater.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
  • Farmers should move livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Rising floodwater can be dangerous. Be aware of water levels rising near you.
  • Floodwater is toxic - never play or swim in floodwater.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.

Impacts in your area:

Impacts in your area:
  • Flooding above floor level of a single-story home is likely to occur in some locations.
  • Snakes, spiders and rats may try to find shelter in your home, even if raised.

This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 03/07/2026 12:30 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.

  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

This Flood Watch is being issued for the the Broken River.

Minor flooding likely in the flood watch area from Thursday. Minor to Moderate flooding occuring in parts of the North East Victoria river catchments

  • Moderate to heavy rainfall recorded across North East Victoria since Monday has caused river level rises and minor to moderate flooding in the Seven and Castle Creeks, and Goulburn River catchments.
  • Further widespread rainfall is forecast across parts of North East Victoria during Thursday and into Friday. 
  • This rainfall may result in minor flooding across the Flood Watch area from Thursday. 
  • Flooding is likely to continue in the Goulburn River, and Seven and Castle Creeks catchments, where Flood Warnings remain current.
  • The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe
Impacts in your area: 
  • For details of flood impacts check out the Local Flood Guide for your area: Greater Shepparton City Council, Benalla Rural City and Moira Shire Council at https://www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
  • Camping areas close to waterways may be flooded.
  • For more information on road closures go to www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 03/07/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes.

properties.name: Watch & Act
properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for Hughes Creek at Tarcombe Road.

Moderate flooding is possible for Seymour and is occurring at Hughes Creek at Tarcombe Road 

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment. Rainfall is forecast to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in further rises and areas of flooding across the Goulburn River catchment.

A Flood Watch is current for Parts of Gippsland, North East and Central Victoria.

This Watch and Act message replaces the Advice issued at 6.45pm 2 July 2026. 
Prepare for flooding now. Take actions to protect your life and property against potential hazards caused by flooding.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
  • Farmers should move livestock and machinery to higher ground.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.
Impacts in your area:
  • Check the VicTraffic website (www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au) for road closures and follow instructions from emergency services.
  • Low lying camping areas may be flooded.
  • High river levels and potential flooding in areas close to rivers and streams. 
  • Heavy rainfall may lead to an increased risk of landslides – be aware of fallen earth, rocks, debris or trees.
  • Main roads and bridges may be closed due to flooding.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 03/07/2026 12:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:

properties.location: the Buffalo River Downstream of Lake Buffalo
properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for the Buffalo River Downstream of Lake Buffalo.

MODERATE FLOODING POSSIBLE DOWNSTREAM OF LAKE BUFFALO EARLY FRIDAY MORNING

Moderate rainfall across the Ovens and King River catchments since Monday has caused significant river level rises.

Minor flooding is occurring along the  Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo. Moderate flooding may develop along the Buffalo River early Friday morning. 

Buffalo River:
Minor flooding is occurring downstream of Lake Buffalo, with moderate flooding possible.

The Buffalo River at Lake Buffalo (Downstream) is currently at 4.73 m and rising, with minor flooding. The Buffalo River at Lake Buffalo (Downstream) may exceed the moderate flood level (5.00 m) Friday morning.The river may reach around 5.20 m Friday afternoon, with moderate flooding.

Stay safe - avoid the flooded area. Be aware of potential hazards caused by flooding and never enter floodwater.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
  • Farmers should move livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • If you are camping close to a creek or river, you may be at risk of flooding and need to move to higher ground, away from waterways.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.

Impacts in your area:

Impacts in your area:
  • Flooding above floor level of a single-story home is likely to occur in some locations.
  • Main roads and bridges may be closed due to flooding.
This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 03/07/2026 11:00 am or as the situation changes.

This Flood Watch is being issued for Campaspe River.

  • In the 24 hours to 9am Thursday, moderate rainfall was observed in the upper Campaspe catchment, including 23mm at Woodend and 18.6mm at Heathcote.
  • As of 11am Thursday, Lake Eppalock was 47.7% full, with negligible downstream flows into the Campaspe River.

properties.webBody:
This Moderate Flood Warning is being issued for Goulburn River Lake Eildon to Seymour.

MODERATE FLOODING POSSIBLE AT SEYMOUR EARLY FRIDAY MORNING

MINOR FLOODING CONTINUING AT TAGGERTY

Moderate rainfall since Monday has caused river and creek level rises in the Goulburn River catchment. Rainfall is forecast to continue through to Friday, which is likely to result in further rises and areas of flooding across the Goulburn River catchment. A Flood Watch is current for Parts of Gippsland, North East and Central Victoria.

Goulburn River Lake Eildon to Seymour:
  • Moderate flooding is possible along the Goulburn River at Seymour.
  • The Goulburn River at Seymour is currently at 4.97 m and rising, with minor flooding. The Goulburn River at Seymour may exceed the moderate flood level (5.20 m) early Friday morning.
Hughes Creek:
  • Moderate flooding is occurring along the Hughes Creek at Tarcombe Road.
Yea River:
  • Minor flooding is occurring at Yea.
King Parrot Creek:
  • River levels are elevated along the King Parrot Creek.
Sunday Creek:
  • Minor flooding is occurring along the Sunday Creek at Tallarook.



This Watch and Act message replaces the Advice Message issued at 6.44pm 2 July 2026. 

Prepare for flooding now. Take actions to protect your life and property against potential hazards caused by flooding.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never enter floodwater.
If you choose to leave:
  • Remember to take your pets, mobile phone, spare clothes and medications.
  • Travel to the home of family or friends who are in a safe location, away from flooding.
  • Turn off gas and electricity at your home or workplace.
  • Be aware of any road closures when you leave.
If you are travelling:
  • Do not enter flooded areas. Turn around a find a safe alternative route.
  • Be aware of road hazards including mud, debris and damaged roads or bridges.
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
If you stay or if it is unsafe to leave:
  • Make sure you have enough food, drinking water, medications and pet food to survive for three to five days in case you become isolated.
  • Use plugs, sandbags or household items to block toilets, sinks and drains to stop sewerage backflow.
  • Lift it - move valuables and vital household items up on high surfaces, such as on top of tables, chairs, benches and beds.
Impacts in your area:
  • Flooding above floor level of a single-story home is likely to occur in some locations.
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
  • Heavy rain over fire affected areas can increase the risk of landslides.  
  • For more information go to: Vic SES website
  • Local roads may be closed and low bridges may be underwater. 
  • Caravan parks and camping grounds may be flooded.
  • For more details or flood impacts check out the Local Flood Guide for Seymour
The following local roads are likely to be closed:
  • Tallarook - Pyalong Road, Tallarook between Sharps Rd, 
  • Taylors Rd and Gerard Rd
  • Delatite Road, Seymour
  • White Gate Road, Seymour - South of Seymour-Pyalong Rd.
  • Ennis Road, Tallarook - Hume Freeway end
  • Ashes Bridge Road, Tallarook

Moderate flooding is occurring along the King River at Docker Road Bridge, with major flooding possible Friday morning. Minor flooding is occurring along the King River downstream of Lake William Hovell and along the Buffalo River downstream of Lake Buffalo. Moderate flooding may develop along the Buffalo River early Friday morning. Moderate flooding is occurring at Cheshunt.

Along the Ovens River, minor flooding is occurring at Bright, Eurobin and Rocky Point, and may develop at Wangaratta early Friday morning.

Minor flooding is occurring along the Buckland River at Harris Lane and along Fifteen Mile Creek at Greta South.

Ovens River downstream of Rocky Point:

Minor flooding is possible at Wangaratta.

The Ovens River at Wangaratta is currently at 11.76 m and rising, below the minor flood level. The Ovens River at Wangaratta may exceed the minor flood level (11.90 m) early Friday morning. The river may reach around 12.20 m Friday afternoon, with minor flooding.

At Minor flood level of 11.9 metres in Wangaratta:
  • APEX Park and farmland along the river floodplains North, South and East of Wangaratta may start to flood.
  • Water may be up to Yogi’s Chin in APEX Park 
  • The carpark at APEX Park, Bickerton St may be closed.
  • Flooding into Sydney Beach carpark commences.
  • Painters Island caravan park has been advised of the possible flooding and have activated their Flood Plan.
At flood level of 12.2 m in Wangaratta:
  • Impacts may be wider spread in Apex Park, reaching the Pavillion and new playground
At Minor flood level of 3.2 metres in Whorouly:
  • Rural flooding commences along the river floodplain

  • This rainfall may result in minor flooding across the Flood Watch area from Thursday. 
  • Flooding is likely to continue in the Goulburn River, and Seven and Castle Creeks catchments, where Flood Warnings remain current.
  • The situation is being closely monitored, and Flood Warnings will be issued as required.
Stay informed - monitor your local conditions and remain alert.
What you should do:
  • Floodwater is dangerous - never drive, walk or ride through floodwater.
  • Farmers should consider moving livestock and machinery to higher ground.
  • Download your Local Flood Guide to understand local flood impacts www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Decide what you and your family will do if flooding impacts you. For information on how to prepare go to www.ses.vic.gov.au/get-ready/floodsafe.
Impacts in your area: 
  • For details of flood impacts check out the Local Flood Guide for your area: Greater Shepparton City Council, Benalla Rural City and Moira Shire Council at https://www.ses.vic.gov.au/plan-and-stay-safe/flood-guides
  • Areas around rivers and streams may be flooded.
  • Camping areas close to waterways may be flooded.
  • For more information on road closures go to www.traffic.transport.vic.gov.au .

This message was issued by State Emergency Service.

The next update is expected by 03/07/2026 02:00 pm or as the situation changes.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfloods)
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
